system_leadership.chief_ai_officer: Chase Rainwater (Provost Fellow for AI, 2025-26; not a formal CAIO title)

key_provisions: Students must have instructor permission to use generative AI in coursework
Users are responsible for accuracy, bias, and appropriateness of AI outputs
Faculty expected to specify AI policies in syllabi
Sensitive/regulated data must not be used with unapproved tools
Exceptions for unapproved tools require formal request
ai_policy.summary: The University of Arkansas launched ai.uark.edu on Aug. 25, 2025, publishing 'Guidelines for Acceptable Use of Artificial Intelligence' organized around four areas: ethical and responsible use, teaching and learning, research, and allowable AI tools. Guidelines were developed by four AI Working Groups (teaching/learning, research, data security, and ethics/training) and apply to students, faculty, and staff.

governance_structure.committee_name: AI Executive Steering Committee / AI Working Groups (coordinated by Provost Fellow for AI)
governance_structure.notes: Structure: (1) AI Task Force created June 2024 by former Provost Terry Martin, led by Chase Rainwater; (2) AI Executive Steering Committee established June 2025; (3) four AI Working Groups established July 2025 covering teaching/learning, research, data security, and ethics/training. In May 2025 Rainwater was named 2025-26 Provost Fellow for AI to implement task force recommendations; he also represents UA on the SEC AI Consortium. Specific working-group rosters are not published on ai.uark.edu.

vendor_deployments.notes: Free to all UARK Microsoft account holders (faculty, staff, graduate assistants). Workshops held March 3-4, 2026.
Requires unit-level license purchase; embedded in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams.
Unit-purchase model; FERPA-aligned data residency. No single campus-wide contract disclosed.
Available to all UARK account holders at no additional cost starting Feb. 2, 2026.
Free for all UARK account holders.

vendor_deployments.product: AI for Education Accelerator (Career Certificates + AI Essentials)
vendor_deployments.notes: U of A joined first cohort of Google's AI for Education Accelerator (100+ public universities). Free AI Essentials course and Google Career Certificates (data analytics, cybersecurity, PM, UX, digital marketing, IT support) for students, faculty, staff. Announced alongside Google's $4B West Memphis data center.

ai_academic_programs.name: AI-focused coursework and offerings at Walton College of Business

notable_deals_investments.title: Google AI for Education Accelerator partnership
notable_deals_investments.summary: Free access to Google AI Essentials and Career Certificates for all students, faculty, and staff; part of Google's $1B investment in AI for education.

notable_deals_investments.title: NSF CAREER Award, Ngan Le (trustworthy multimodal AI for video analytics)
notable_deals_investments.summary: Associate professor in EECS awarded NSF CAREER to develop biologically inspired, energy-efficient multimodal AI frameworks.

leadership_statements.speaker: Indrajeet Chaubey
leadership_statements.role: Provost and Executive Vice Chancellor for Academic Affairs, University of Arkansas
leadership_statements.context: Announcement of Google AI for Education Accelerator partnership
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: 'Fulfilling our land-grant mission means ensuring that the education and training we offer keep pace with technological innovation.' Noted certificates are 'designed by industry leaders, aligned with real employer needs.'

state_ai_policy_context.summary: Governor Sarah Huckabee Sanders has launched an AI Working Group and established an Arkansas AI and Analytics Center of Excellence, and in Feb 2026 issued an executive order directing state agencies to use AI to expedite permitting. Sanders has publicly opposed federal AI regulatory moratoriums. The University of Arkansas System Board of Trustees heard AI updates at its Aug 2025 retreat but has not adopted a single system-wide AI policy; each campus (UAF, UALR, etc.) is developing its own guidelines, with UAF releasing acceptable-use guidance in Aug 2025. Existing academic-integrity and data-security policies are being leveraged to cover AI.

narrative_summary: The University of Arkansas (Fayetteville) has moved from ad-hoc experimentation to a structured, provost-driven AI posture over 2024-2026. A provost-appointed AI Task Force (June 2024, led by industrial engineering professor Chase Rainwater) produced recommendations that matured into an AI Executive Steering Committee (June 2025) and four AI Working Groups (July 2025) covering teaching/learning, research, data security, and ethics/training. Rainwater was named 2025-26 Provost Fellow for AI and is UA's representative to the SEC AI Consortium, but there is no formal Chief AI Officer title. Acceptable-use guidelines went live at ai.uark.edu on Aug 25, 2025. UAF's vendor stack is multi-modal and pragmatic: Microsoft Copilot Chat, Google Gemini, and Google NotebookLM are campus-wide at no additional cost; Copilot for M365 and ChatGPT Edu are available on a unit-purchase model rather than a single enterprise contract; Claude for Education is notably absent from the approved list. A November 2025 partnership joined UA to Google's AI for Education Accelerator, providing free AI Essentials and Career Certificates. The UA System Board of Trustees has been briefed but has not issued a system-wide AI policy. State-level context: Governor Sanders has established an AI and Analytics Center of Excellence and issued AI-related executive orders focused on state-agency efficiency; she has publicly opposed federal AI regulatory moratoriums, signaling a pro-deployment state stance that gives UA cover to move quickly.
confidence_notes: HIGH confidence on: policy adoption, governance structure, approved vendor list, CIO identity, state AI posture, Google partnership. MEDIUM confidence on: scope of ChatGPT Edu and Copilot M365 deployments (unit-level, no disclosed user counts or contract values). LOW/GAP: no published CAIO title (Rainwater is Provost Fellow, not CAIO); no disclosed Claude for Education deployment; no specific AI degree programs at UAF identified (AI is embedded in Walton College and CS/EECS but no standalone BS in AI at Fayetteville; UA Little Rock is launching a BS in AI separately); no public working-group rosters; Chancellor Robinson has no AI-specific public statement located in searches; arkansasonline.com paywall blocked direct fetch of UA System trustees AI update article.
